processing_families.title: Sample Preparation
processing_families.summary: Build an analysis-ready sample before cleaning, plotting, or model estimation.
processing_families.description: Use this family when the main question is whether the sample itself is well-formed. It focuses on variable inclusion, required fields, numeric and binary coercion, date parsing, and row eligibility rules.
key_inputs: Keep columns and required columns
Force numeric, binary, or date parsing
Duplicate-row removal and missing-value drop rules
manual_checks: Confirm every required field survives after type coercion.
Recount dropped rows against the raw file before moving to modeling.
Verify binary and date columns against the original labels in the preview table.

methods.name: Column Selection
methods.description: Restrict the working sample to the columns needed for the research design.

methods.name: Required Field Filter
methods.description: Drop observations that do not contain the minimum fields required for identification.

methods.name: Type Coercion
methods.description: Force variables into numeric, binary, or date types before downstream transformations.

methods.name: Duplicate and Missing Rules
methods.description: Apply explicit duplicate-row removal and missing-value policies before saving the prepared sample.

variant_presets.label: Strict row eligibility
variant_presets.description: Keep duplicate removal and required-field filtering on so the prepared sample is maximally clean before modeling.

variant_presets.label: Loose eligibility review
variant_presets.description: Temporarily keep duplicate and missing-row rules off so you can inspect borderline rows before imposing final eligibility filters.

processing_families.title: Cleaning & Transforms
processing_families.summary: Normalize variables, cap extremes, and generate transformed regressors with explicit thresholds.
processing_families.description: Use this family when the raw sample already exists but its scale, outliers, or missingness would distort inference. The point is to make every cleaning step explicit and reproducible.
key_inputs: Imputation method and target columns
Winsorization bounds and columns
Log transform, standardization, min-max scaling, and outlier thresholds
manual_checks: Recompute each transformed column from the downloaded prepared sample.
Check that winsorized variables do not exceed the declared quantile bounds.
Compare the transformed preview rows against a manual spreadsheet calculation.

methods.name: Imputation
methods.description: Fill missing values with mean, median, zero, forward-fill, or backward-fill rules.

methods.name: Winsorization
methods.description: Clip tail observations using transparent lower and upper quantile thresholds.

methods.name: Log, Standardize, Min-Max
methods.description: Generate comparable scales for regression and charting workflows.

methods.name: Outlier Filters
methods.description: Remove extreme values using IQR or z-score rules with explicit cutoffs.

variant_presets.label: Median + conservative tails
variant_presets.description: Median imputation with conservative winsor bounds and IQR outlier filtering.

variant_presets.label: Mean + z-score trim
variant_presets.description: Mean imputation with wider tails and z-score outlier trimming for a looser robustness pass.

processing_families.title: Time-Series Features
processing_families.summary: Generate differences, returns, lags, leads, and rolling diagnostics before time-series or finance models.
processing_families.description: Use this family when the identification or forecasting logic depends on ordered observations. It exposes the exact sort variable, grouping variable, and transformation windows used to construct derived series.
key_inputs: Sort column and optional panel/time grouping column
Difference, return, lag, and lead targets
Rolling mean and rolling volatility windows
manual_checks: Verify the sort order before recomputing returns or lags.
Rebuild at least one derived series by hand to confirm the grouping logic.
Check that rolling windows use the documented horizon and missing-value behavior.

methods.name: Differences
methods.description: Create first differences or grouped differences for trend-stationary analysis.

methods.name: Simple and Log Returns
methods.description: Construct finance-style return series from prices or levels using transparent formulas.

methods.name: Lags and Leads
methods.description: Create dynamic explanatory variables for event studies, ARIMA, VAR, and panel models.

methods.name: Rolling Statistics
methods.description: Compute rolling means and volatility windows for macro-finance diagnostics.

variant_presets.label: Short-horizon returns
variant_presets.description: Use log returns with one-step lags/leads and a short rolling window for monthly-style macro-finance panels.

variant_presets.label: Longer smoothing window
variant_presets.description: Keep simple returns but use longer dynamic windows for slower-moving macro variables.

processing_families.title: Visualization
processing_families.summary: Render line, scatter, bar, and histogram charts and export them as PNG assets.
processing_families.description: Use this family when the immediate goal is to inspect the shape of the data, validate a transformation, or produce a transparent figure that can be downloaded and checked independently.
key_inputs: Chart type, x-variable, and y-variable selection
Optional group/color dimension
Exportable PNG output with direct download
manual_checks: Confirm plotted columns match the selected variables in the chart form.
Compare chart points against the downloaded source sample or prepared sample.
Download the PNG and verify its title, axes, and grouping choices before reuse.

methods.name: Line Chart
methods.description: Inspect trajectories, trends, and series comovement over ordered observations.

methods.name: Scatter Chart
methods.description: Check bivariate relationships, clustering, and possible nonlinear patterns.

methods.name: Bar Chart
methods.description: Compare grouped levels, shares, or event counts across categories.

methods.name: Histogram
methods.description: Inspect empirical distributions before choosing transformations or tail-risk models.

variant_presets.label: High-resolution export
variant_presets.description: Use a denser chart output budget so diagnostics retain more points before export.

variant_presets.label: Compact review chart
variant_presets.description: Keep chart exports lighter for quick manual review and notebook-style iteration.

model_families.title: Econometrics Baseline
model_families.summary: Classical empirical economics workflows for treatment effects, structural breaks, and panel-style identification.
model_families.description: This family groups the baseline empirical designs most users need first: linear regression, binary response models, treatment-effect estimators, fixed effects, gravity, and IV designs.
key_inputs: Outcome variable and explanatory variables
Treatment, post, running, entity, time, endogenous, and instrument fields when applicable
Robust covariance toggle and transparent coefficient output
manual_checks: Rebuild the design matrix from the prepared sample and compare term order with the coefficient table.
Check treatment, post, running, or instrument fields directly in the downloaded sample.
Re-estimate at least one specification externally using the same covariance setting.

methods.name: OLS
methods.description: Baseline linear model for average partial effects.

variant_presets.label: Robust covariance
variant_presets.description: Use heteroskedasticity-robust covariance as the default empirical baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical covariance
variant_presets.description: Re-run the same design with classical covariance for a direct table-to-table comparison.

methods.name: PPML
methods.description: Poisson pseudo-maximum likelihood for nonnegative outcomes and flow data.

variant_presets.label: Robust covariance
variant_presets.description: Use heteroskedasticity-robust covariance as the default empirical baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical covariance
variant_presets.description: Re-run the same design with classical covariance for a direct table-to-table comparison.

methods.name: Logit
methods.description: Binary response model with logistic link.

variant_presets.label: Robust covariance
variant_presets.description: Use heteroskedasticity-robust covariance as the default empirical baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical covariance
variant_presets.description: Re-run the same design with classical covariance for a direct table-to-table comparison.

methods.name: Probit
methods.description: Binary response model with normal link.

variant_presets.label: Robust covariance
variant_presets.description: Use heteroskedasticity-robust covariance as the default empirical baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical covariance
variant_presets.description: Re-run the same design with classical covariance for a direct table-to-table comparison.

methods.name: Difference-in-Differences
methods.description: Two-group/two-period style treatment-effect estimation.

variant_presets.label: Robust DID baseline
variant_presets.description: Keep robust covariance for the main DID table.

variant_presets.label: Classical DID comparison
variant_presets.description: Turn robust covariance off for a table-to-table comparison against the baseline.

methods.name: Event Study
methods.description: Dynamic treatment effects around an event window.

variant_presets.label: Narrow event window
variant_presets.description: Use a tighter lead-lag window for compact treatment dynamics.

variant_presets.label: Wide event window
variant_presets.description: Use a wider event window to inspect longer-run anticipation and adjustment paths.

methods.name: Regression Discontinuity
methods.description: Local identification around an observed cutoff.

variant_presets.label: Local linear RDD
variant_presets.description: Estimate the discontinuity with a local linear specification.

variant_presets.label: Quadratic bandwidth check
variant_presets.description: Expand the bandwidth and fit a quadratic polynomial for robustness.

methods.name: Fixed Effects
methods.description: Entity and optional time fixed-effects estimation.

variant_presets.label: Entity effects only
variant_presets.description: Estimate with entity effects only.

variant_presets.label: Two-way effects
variant_presets.description: Add time effects for a two-way fixed-effects robustness check.

methods.name: Gravity Model
methods.description: Trade- and flow-style models using mass and distance terms.

variant_presets.label: Robust gravity
variant_presets.description: Run the gravity model with robust covariance.

variant_presets.label: Classical gravity
variant_presets.description: Run the same gravity design with classical covariance for comparison.

methods.name: IV-2SLS
methods.description: Instrumental-variables regression for endogeneity correction.

variant_presets.label: Robust IV
variant_presets.description: Use robust covariance in the 2SLS baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical IV
variant_presets.description: Turn robust covariance off to compare with conventional 2SLS output.

methods.name: Panel IV
methods.description: Panel-style IV with entity and time structure.

variant_presets.label: Entity IV
variant_presets.description: Use panel IV without time effects.

variant_presets.label: Two-way panel IV
variant_presets.description: Add time effects to the panel IV setup.

methods.name: Random Effects
methods.description: Panel random-effects estimator for partially pooled entity variation.

methods.name: First Difference
methods.description: Panel first-difference estimator that removes time-invariant heterogeneity.

methods.name: Between OLS
methods.description: Between estimator using unit-level averages across time.

methods.name: Pooled OLS
methods.description: Pooled panel regression benchmark without fixed effects.

methods.name: Fama-MacBeth
methods.description: Cross-sectional asset-pricing regression with time-series aggregation of coefficients.

methods.name: IV-LIML
methods.description: Limited-information maximum likelihood estimator for weak-instrument settings.

methods.name: IV-GMM
methods.description: Generalized-method-of-moments IV estimator with over-identification diagnostics.

methods.name: Absorbing Least Squares
methods.description: High-dimensional fixed-effects regression using absorbed dummies.

methods.name: SUR
methods.description: Seemingly unrelated regression for correlated system equations.

methods.name: IV-3SLS
methods.description: Three-stage least squares for simultaneous-equation systems with instruments.

methods.name: System GMM
methods.description: System-GMM style estimator for dynamic panel equations.

methods.name: GLM
methods.description: Generalized linear model with configurable family and link.

methods.name: Quantile Regression
methods.description: Estimate conditional quantiles instead of the conditional mean.

methods.name: GEE
methods.description: Generalized estimating equations for correlated outcomes.

methods.name: Multinomial Logit
methods.description: Multinomial discrete-choice model for outcomes with more than two categories.

methods.name: Negative Binomial
methods.description: Over-dispersed count model with explicit dispersion parameter.

methods.name: Zero-Inflated Count
methods.description: Zero-inflated Poisson or negative-binomial count specification.

methods.name: Mixed Linear Model
methods.description: Linear mixed-effects model with group-specific random structure.

variant_presets.label: Robust covariance
variant_presets.description: Use heteroskedasticity-robust covariance as the default empirical baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical covariance
variant_presets.description: Re-run the same design with classical covariance for a direct table-to-table comparison.

model_families.title: Time Series & Econometric Finance
model_families.summary: Forecasting, volatility, impulse-response, and connectedness models for ordered macro-finance data.
model_families.description: Use this family when time ordering is central to the question. These models cover univariate forecasting, volatility clustering, multivariate dynamic interactions, recursive structural impulse responses, and spillover-connectedness diagnostics.
key_inputs: Time column
Series variable or multivariate series selection
Lag order, ARIMA / ARCH / GARCH order, horizon, and optional recursive shock ordering
manual_checks: Confirm the time column sorts correctly before estimation.
Recompute lagged design terms or volatility recursions in the downloaded sample.
Verify forecast horizons, lag order, and recursive ordering assumptions against the model specification panel.

methods.name: ARIMA Forecast
methods.description: Univariate forecasting with explicit ARIMA(p,d,q) inputs.

variant_presets.label: ARIMA short-memory
variant_presets.description: Use a compact ARIMA(1,0,1) configuration with short forecast steps.

variant_presets.label: ARIMA differenced trend
variant_presets.description: Difference once and extend the forecast horizon for persistence checks.

methods.name: ARCH
methods.description: Conditional heteroskedasticity model for volatility clustering in return series.

variant_presets.label: ARCH(1) baseline
variant_presets.description: A simple ARCH-style volatility specification.

variant_presets.label: ARCH(2) check
variant_presets.description: Increase ARCH order for a sensitivity pass on short volatility clustering.

methods.name: GARCH
methods.description: Generalized ARCH model for persistent conditional variance dynamics.

variant_presets.label: GARCH(1,1) benchmark
variant_presets.description: Use the canonical GARCH(1,1) specification.

variant_presets.label: GARCH(2,1) persistence check
variant_presets.description: Increase the ARCH order and forecast horizon for a persistence comparison.

methods.name: Vector Autoregression
methods.description: Multivariate dynamic system for macro-finance spillovers.

variant_presets.label: VAR short lag
variant_presets.description: Use one lag and a shorter forecast window.

variant_presets.label: VAR longer lag
variant_presets.description: Use two lags and extend the forecast horizon for a richer dynamic comparison.

methods.name: SVAR IRF
methods.description: Recursive-identified structural impulse responses and cumulative response paths.

variant_presets.label: SVAR compact IRF
variant_presets.description: Use a shorter horizon for compact impulse-response tables and figures.

variant_presets.label: SVAR extended IRF
variant_presets.description: Use more lags and a longer horizon to inspect persistent structural responses.

methods.name: VIRF
methods.description: Volatility impulse response path implied by a fitted GARCH(1,1) process.

variant_presets.label: One-sigma shock
variant_presets.description: Trace the volatility impulse response from a one-sigma shock.

variant_presets.label: Two-sigma shock
variant_presets.description: Increase the shock size to inspect stronger volatility propagation.

methods.name: DY Connectedness
methods.description: Diebold-Yilmaz spillover table and total connectedness index from generalized FEVD.

variant_presets.label: Short spillover window
variant_presets.description: Use a compact connectedness horizon with one lag.

variant_presets.label: Extended spillover window
variant_presets.description: Extend both lag order and horizon for longer connectedness diagnostics.

methods.name: BK Connectedness
methods.description: Barunik-Krehlik frequency connectedness decomposition across short, medium, and long horizons.

variant_presets.label: Short/medium frequency split
variant_presets.description: Use tighter short and medium frequency cutoffs.

variant_presets.label: Longer frequency split
variant_presets.description: Expand the BK horizons to emphasize slower cyclical connectedness.

methods.name: VARMAX
methods.description: State-space VARMAX model for multivariate dynamics with moving-average terms.

methods.name: VECM
methods.description: Vector error-correction model for cointegrated multivariate systems.

methods.name: Markov Switching
methods.description: Regime-switching model with latent states and filtered probabilities.

methods.name: Unobserved Components
methods.description: State-space decomposition into trend, cycle, and irregular components.

methods.name: Exponential Smoothing
methods.description: ETS forecast model with level, trend, and seasonality controls.

methods.name: EGARCH
methods.description: Exponential GARCH model for asymmetric volatility responses.

methods.name: GJR-GARCH
methods.description: Threshold GARCH model with leverage effects.

methods.name: HARX
methods.description: Heterogeneous autoregressive model with exogenous features for realized-volatility style problems.

methods.name: ADF Unit Root Test
methods.description: Augmented Dickey-Fuller unit root test with lag and trend options.

methods.name: KPSS Stationarity Test
methods.description: KPSS stationarity test with level or trend null.

methods.name: Phillips-Perron Test
methods.description: Phillips-Perron unit root test with heteroskedasticity-robust correction.

methods.name: Zivot-Andrews Break Test
methods.description: Unit root test with a single endogenous structural break.

methods.name: Engle-Granger Cointegration
methods.description: Residual-based cointegration test for long-run equilibrium relationships.

methods.name: Dynamic OLS
methods.description: Cointegration estimator with leads and lags of first differences.

methods.name: Fully Modified OLS
methods.description: Cointegration estimator robust to endogeneity and serial correlation.

variant_presets.label: Short horizon
variant_presets.description: Use a short forecast and impulse-response horizon with one lag.

variant_presets.label: Long horizon
variant_presets.description: Stretch the dynamic horizon and lag order to inspect persistence and longer spillovers.

model_families.title: Corporate Finance
model_families.summary: Transparent ratio-based diagnostics for firm health and profitability structure.
model_families.description: Use this family when the main need is interpretability rather than structural estimation. It exposes each financial ratio and lets users cross-check the accounting arithmetic directly.
key_inputs: Working capital, retained earnings, EBIT, market equity, assets, liabilities, sales
Net income, revenue, and equity for DuPont decomposition
manual_checks: Recompute every ratio from the raw firm-level columns.
Check sign conventions and units before interpreting score thresholds.
Compare the displayed metrics against a manual spreadsheet calculation.

methods.name: Altman Z-Score
methods.description: Firm distress screening using the classic Z-Score ratio combination.

variant_presets.label: Balance-sheet focus
variant_presets.description: A bookkeeping-focused corporate-finance pass that keeps the emphasis on balance-sheet variables.

methods.name: DuPont Analysis
methods.description: Profit margin, asset turnover, and leverage decomposition of ROE.

variant_presets.label: Balance-sheet focus
variant_presets.description: A bookkeeping-focused corporate-finance pass that keeps the emphasis on balance-sheet variables.

variant_presets.label: Balance-sheet focus
variant_presets.description: A bookkeeping-focused corporate-finance pass that keeps the emphasis on balance-sheet variables.

model_families.title: Risk Management
model_families.summary: Loss and volatility diagnostics for market risk workflows.
model_families.description: Use this family when the focus is downside risk, volatility persistence, or stress-style tail diagnostics rather than causal inference.
key_inputs: Return or loss series
Confidence level and holding period
EWMA decay parameter for volatility tracking
manual_checks: Recompute the empirical tail cutoff from the downloaded sample.
Check confidence level and holding-period scaling before comparing VaR numbers.
Verify EWMA recursion against a hand-built spreadsheet for at least several rows.

methods.name: Historical VaR / ES
methods.description: Nonparametric tail-risk calculation from the empirical distribution.

variant_presets.label: Tighter confidence
variant_presets.description: Estimate risk metrics at a 99 percent confidence level.

variant_presets.label: Multi-day horizon
variant_presets.description: Expand the holding period to inspect longer horizon loss aggregation.

methods.name: Parametric VaR / ES
methods.description: Normal-style VaR and expected shortfall from estimated mean and volatility.

variant_presets.label: Tighter confidence
variant_presets.description: Estimate risk metrics at a 99 percent confidence level.

variant_presets.label: Multi-day horizon
variant_presets.description: Expand the holding period to inspect longer horizon loss aggregation.

methods.name: EWMA Volatility
methods.description: Exponentially weighted volatility dynamics for recent-history emphasis.

variant_presets.label: Baseline lambda
variant_presets.description: Use the canonical RiskMetrics-style lambda.

variant_presets.label: Faster decay
variant_presets.description: Reduce lambda to emphasize recent shocks more aggressively.

variant_presets.label: Tighter confidence
variant_presets.description: Estimate risk metrics at a 99 percent confidence level.

variant_presets.label: Multi-day horizon
variant_presets.description: Expand the holding period to inspect longer horizon loss aggregation.

model_families.title: Derivatives Pricing
model_families.summary: Vanilla option-pricing workflows with transparent parameter exposure.
model_families.description: Use this family for direct pricing of standard European-style calls and puts. The page shows all pricing inputs so the result can be checked against a calculator or spreadsheet.
key_inputs: Spot, strike, time to maturity, rate, and volatility inputs
Option type and binomial step count
manual_checks: Verify the units of maturity and volatility before comparing prices.
Check the sign and scale of the risk-free rate.
Reproduce the price with an independent Black-Scholes or binomial calculator.

methods.name: Black-Scholes
methods.description: Closed-form pricing for European vanilla options.

variant_presets.label: Call baseline
variant_presets.description: Keep call-option pricing with a moderate binomial tree depth.

variant_presets.label: Put robustness
variant_presets.description: Re-price with a put payoff and a denser tree for a robustness comparison.

methods.name: Binomial Option Pricing
methods.description: Discrete-time lattice pricing with user-specified step count.

variant_presets.label: Call baseline
variant_presets.description: Keep call-option pricing with a moderate binomial tree depth.

variant_presets.label: Put robustness
variant_presets.description: Re-price with a put payoff and a denser tree for a robustness comparison.

variant_presets.label: Call baseline
variant_presets.description: Keep call-option pricing with a moderate binomial tree depth.

variant_presets.label: Put robustness
variant_presets.description: Re-price with a put payoff and a denser tree for a robustness comparison.

model_families.title: Macro Finance & DSGE
model_families.summary: Policy-rule diagnostics and a lightweight dynamic general-equilibrium sandbox.
model_families.description: Use this family when the question is macro-policy oriented. It exposes simple policy-rule and toy equilibrium parameters transparently rather than hiding them behind a black-box calibration.
key_inputs: Inflation gap and output gap variables for Taylor-rule estimation
RBC/DSGE preference, technology, depreciation, and shock parameters
manual_checks: Confirm gap variables are already constructed consistently with the research design.
Recompute the Taylor-rule regression externally with the same specification.
For the toy DSGE module, verify calibration inputs and impulse horizon manually.

methods.name: Taylor Rule
methods.description: Transparent reduced-form policy-rule regression.

variant_presets.label: Baseline policy rule
variant_presets.description: Keep the baseline Taylor-rule style specification.

variant_presets.label: Classical policy rule
variant_presets.description: Use classical covariance for a specification comparison.

methods.name: Toy RBC / DSGE
methods.description: A simple calibration-style dynamic general-equilibrium sandbox.

variant_presets.label: Moderate persistence
variant_presets.description: Use a moderate shock persistence and horizon suitable for a first DSGE-style impulse pass.

variant_presets.label: High persistence
variant_presets.description: Increase shock persistence and horizon to inspect more durable macro propagation.

model_families.title: Portfolio Allocation
model_families.summary: Weight-construction workflows for mean-variance, minimum-variance, and risk-parity portfolios.
model_families.description: Use this family when the output of interest is portfolio weights rather than a coefficient table. The emphasis is on explicit return inputs, risk preferences, and weight vectors that can be checked manually.
key_inputs: Return series for each asset
Risk-aversion and long-only choice
manual_checks: Rebuild the sample covariance matrix from the prepared sample.
Verify the optimizer constraints, especially long-only assumptions.
Check that final weights sum to one and match the reported allocation table.

methods.name: Mean-Variance Portfolio
methods.description: Classic risk-return frontier allocation using expected returns and covariances.

variant_presets.label: Long-only benchmark
variant_presets.description: Keep the portfolio constrained to long-only allocations.

variant_presets.label: Flexible allocation
variant_presets.description: Relax the long-only constraint and reduce risk aversion for a more aggressive allocation design.

methods.name: Minimum Variance Portfolio
methods.description: Variance-minimizing allocation under the chosen constraint set.

variant_presets.label: Long-only benchmark
variant_presets.description: Keep the portfolio constrained to long-only allocations.

variant_presets.label: Flexible allocation
variant_presets.description: Relax the long-only constraint and reduce risk aversion for a more aggressive allocation design.

methods.name: Risk Parity Portfolio
methods.description: Allocation that balances risk contribution across assets.

variant_presets.label: Long-only benchmark
variant_presets.description: Keep the portfolio constrained to long-only allocations.

variant_presets.label: Flexible allocation
variant_presets.description: Relax the long-only constraint and reduce risk aversion for a more aggressive allocation design.

methods.name: Efficient Frontier
methods.description: Mean-variance frontier and optimal portfolio under configurable objectives.

methods.name: Efficient Semivariance
methods.description: Downside-risk frontier using semivariance instead of total variance.

methods.name: Efficient CVaR
methods.description: Tail-risk-aware allocation using CVaR optimization.

methods.name: Black-Litterman
methods.description: Bayesian portfolio combination of equilibrium returns and user views.

methods.name: Hierarchical Risk Parity
methods.description: Clustering-based portfolio construction with hierarchical risk parity.

methods.name: Discrete Allocation
methods.description: Translate continuous weights into integer holdings for implementable portfolios.

methods.name: CDaR Frontier
methods.description: Portfolio optimization using Conditional Drawdown at Risk as the risk objective.

variant_presets.label: Long-only benchmark
variant_presets.description: Keep the portfolio constrained to long-only allocations.

variant_presets.label: Flexible allocation
variant_presets.description: Relax the long-only constraint and reduce risk aversion for a more aggressive allocation design.

model_families.title: Asset Pricing
model_families.summary: Factor-based return regressions with exposed excess-return construction.
model_families.description: Use this family when the focus is factor exposure and expected-return decomposition. The page keeps factor construction explicit so users can verify each excess-return series before estimation.
key_inputs: Asset return series, market factor, and risk-free rate
SMB and HML factors for the Fama-French specification
manual_checks: Rebuild asset excess returns and market excess returns from the downloaded sample.
Check factor alignment and frequency before comparing alphas and betas.
Re-estimate the regression externally and compare coefficients term by term.

methods.name: CAPM
methods.description: Single-factor pricing regression with market excess returns.

variant_presets.label: Robust factor regression
variant_presets.description: Use robust covariance in the asset-pricing regression baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical factor regression
variant_presets.description: Re-run the factor regression with classical covariance for comparison against robust inference.

methods.name: Fama-French 3-Factor
methods.description: Multi-factor pricing regression with SMB and HML exposures.

methods.name: Traded Factor Model
methods.description: Linear factor model for traded factors with alpha and risk-premia diagnostics.

methods.name: Linear Factor GMM
methods.description: GMM-based linear factor pricing model for non-traded or estimated factors.

variant_presets.label: Robust factor regression
variant_presets.description: Use robust covariance in the asset-pricing regression baseline.

variant_presets.label: Classical factor regression
variant_presets.description: Re-run the factor regression with classical covariance for comparison against robust inference.

model_families.title: Causal Inference
model_families.summary: Policy-evaluation and design-based causal estimators with explicit identification checks.
model_families.description: Use this family for quasi-experimental designs such as staggered adoption, synthetic controls, interrupted time series, and regression-kink style identification.
key_inputs: Outcome, treatment, time, design variables
Donor pool or pre-treatment structure
Placebo and pre-trend settings
manual_checks: Confirm treatment timing definitions.
Inspect donor/pre-period balance.
Validate placebo or sensitivity outputs against the design narrative.

methods.name: Staggered DID
methods.description: Difference-in-differences with staggered adoption timing and cohort-time effects.

methods.name: Synthetic Control
methods.description: Construct a synthetic counterfactual from donor units for case-study treatment analysis.

methods.name: Interrupted Time Series
methods.description: Estimate level and slope changes after a policy interruption in ordered data.

methods.name: Regression Kink
methods.description: Causal identification from slope changes at a known kink point.

methods.name: Instrumental Variable Causal
methods.description: CausalPy IV workflow with design-oriented diagnostics.

methods.name: Inverse Propensity Weighting
methods.description: Reweight observational samples using estimated treatment propensities.

model_families.title: Bayesian Modeling
model_families.summary: Bayesian regression, causal, and panel models with posterior diagnostics.
model_families.description: Use this family when posterior uncertainty, priors, or hierarchical structure is the primary research requirement.
key_inputs: Outcome and predictor variables
Prior settings and sampler configuration
Posterior predictive checks
manual_checks: Inspect trace diagnostics and divergences.
Check prior sensitivity against posterior summaries.
Verify HDI and predictive plots against posterior tables.

methods.name: Bayesian Linear Regression
methods.description: Linear regression estimated with priors and posterior inference.

methods.name: Bayesian Panel
methods.description: Hierarchical panel regression with unit-level random structure.

methods.name: Bayesian DID
methods.description: Bayesian treatment-effect estimation for before-after designs.

methods.name: Bayesian ITS
methods.description: Bayesian interrupted time-series with posterior intervention effects.

model_families.title: Quant Research
model_families.summary: Prediction, signal evaluation, and lightweight backtest workflows inspired by qlib.
model_families.description: Use this family for predictive alpha research and strategy diagnostics within the current workspace, without depending on qlib online services.
key_inputs: Feature columns
Label horizon
Backtest or ranking configuration
manual_checks: Verify feature/label definitions.
Check train-test split dates.
